name: European Portuguese

allophone_rules.notes: Velarised (dark) coda /l/ -> [ɫ]. In European Portuguese /l/ is velarised in the syllable coda (word-finally and pre-consonantally: sol, alto, mel) while onset /l/ stays clear. Mateus & d'Andrade (2000: ch.2); Cruz-Ferreira (1995: 91). This is the post-lexical coda realisation that the pre-lexical positional 'l' map does not reach word-finally.

Coda sibilant palatalisation /s/ -> [ʃ] (the 'chiado'): the Lisbon/standard European Portuguese realisation of a coda /s/ (word-final and pre-consonantal: as, pasta). Voicing before a voiced consonant ([ʃ]->[ʒ]) is handled by the PT_CODA_S_VOICING sandhi rule. Mateus & d'Andrade (2000: ch.2); Cruz-Ferreira (1995: 91). This is the Lisbon-standard value; some northern/insular dialects keep an apico-alveolar [s̺] in coda, which is a dialect delta and not modelled in the base.
Voiced counterpart of PT_CODA_S_HUSH: a coda /z/ -> [ʒ] in Lisbon/standard European Portuguese. Mateus & d'Andrade (2000: ch.2).

notes: Modern European Portuguese (PT-PT). Base mapping uses a neutral orthographic standard. Digraphs per Acordo Ortográfico. Portuguese preserves the /v/~/b/ distinction (unique in Ibero-Romance — Castilian merged them). Nasal vowels and extreme vowel reduction are diagnostic features. Post-lexical allophony: the diagnostic EP unstressed vowel reduction (unstressed /a/->[ɐ], /e/->[ɨ] (word-final often deletable), /o/->[u]) is realised in the PRE-lexical positional_graphemes map (the nucleus_stressed/nucleus_unstressed/pretonic/posttonic/word_final rows), so no redundant allophone rule restates it. The allophone_rules layer adds the post-lexical CONSONANT realisations that the grapheme map cannot reach cleanly at word edges: dark coda /l/->[ɫ], and the Lisbon-standard coda sibilants /s/->[ʃ], /z/->[ʒ] (the 'chiado'). Intervocalic voiced-stop spirantisation (/b d ɡ/->[β ð ɣ]; Mateus & d'Andrade 2000) is a genuine EP process but is DELIBERATELY NOT encoded here: every available pt gold (infopedia_pt, ep_dialects, wikipron) is broad/phonemic and transcribes the stops, so a spirantisation rule is correct yet regresses all measurable golds with none rewarding it; it is left for a future narrow-transcription task. Dialect-specific sibilant/vocalism deltas (northern apico-alveolar coda [s̺], insular vocalism) are handled by the pt-PT-x-* dialect specs, not the base. Benchmark honesty: the coda sibilant rules improve every pt gold; the dark-l rule improves the narrow human golds (infopedia_pt, ep_dialects) but regresses the broader WikiPron pt row (which transcribes coda /l/ as plain [l]) — it is kept per the honesty gate because the higher-quality golds reward it, with the divergence documented.

sources.author: Cruz-Ferreira, Madalena
sources.title: European Portuguese
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 25(2), 90-94 (Illustrations of the IPA), Cambridge University Press
sources.notes: Illustration of the IPA for European (Lisbon-standard) Portuguese: velarised coda /l/ [ɫ], coda sibilant [ʃ ʒ], unstressed vowel reduction.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
